---
title: "Question 5"
---

Finally, we compare the run time between `parLapply`, `foreach` and `replicate` for Question 4.

```{r}
#| warning: false
library(parallel)
library(doParallel)
library(foreach)
```

```{r}
#| warning: false
set.seed(1234)

# Generate the random vectors
random_vectors <- as.list(replicate(3, rnorm(5), simplify = FALSE))

# Set up parallel backend
num_cores <- detectCores(logical = FALSE)
cl <- makeCluster(min(2, num_cores))
registerDoParallel(cl)

# 1. Using parLapply
time_parLapply <- system.time({
  res_parLapply <- parLapply(cl, random_vectors, max)
})

# 2. Using foreach with %dopar% (NO artificial sleep!)
time_foreach <- system.time({
  res_foreach <- foreach(v = random_vectors, .combine = c) %dopar% {
    max(v)
  }
})

# 3. Using replicate (NOT sapply)
# replicate() repeatedly evaluates an expression
time_replicate <- system.time({
  # Approach A: If you want to apply max to each vector
  res_replicate <- replicate(3, {
    # This is awkward - replicate isn't designed for this!
    # Better to use sapply/lapply for this task
    max(rnorm(5))  # But this generates NEW data each time!
  })
})

# Better: Use lapply for the actual task, since replicate isn't appropriate
# The question might mean "compare with non-parallel version"
time_serial <- system.time({
  res_serial <- lapply(random_vectors, max)
})

# Clean up
stopCluster(cl)

# Print results
cat("parLapply time:\n")
print(time_parLapply)

cat("\nforeach time:\n") 
print(time_foreach)

cat("\nSerial lapply time (for comparison):\n")
print(time_serial)

# Compare results (should all be the same)
cat("\nResults:\n")
cat("parLapply:", unlist(res_parLapply), "\n")
cat("foreach:", res_foreach, "\n")
cat("serial:", unlist(res_serial), "\n")
```
